This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_label_warning_resolution.reason: SA Stage B strict tier was rebuilt from deterministic strict-mode engine findings; raw Azure auto-label warnings no longer describe active must_detect labels. Broader statutory coverage remains in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

must_detect.matched_text:
Term Sheet — Strategic Acquisition and Financing of Najmah Cloud Technologies JSC (in incorporation) by Zawaya Capital SPC — Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
Parties: Buyer Zawaya Capital SPC (fictional), Seller/Founder Najmah Cloud Technologies JSC (draft CR: 7X3‑9■‑KSA); Contacts: Mr. Sami Al‑Najdi, m.alnajdi@zawaya.example, +966‑5•••‑3812; Dr. Rami Al‑Qassim (Legal), r.alqassim@najmah.example
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 60% of Najmah for SAR 85,000,000 (cash and vendor loan); intended listing on Rimal Exchange Growth Market within 12 months post‑closing; bookrunner to be appointed; no public offer is made by this term sheet
Conditions precedent: Kingdom Markets Authority (KMA) clearance for merger exemptions; issuer onboarding forms for Rimal Exchange completed; no Material Adverse Change has occurred and none is presently expected (benign statement for precision); long‑stop 2026‑09‑30
Confidentiality and MNPI: Parties acknowledge MNPI restrictions per KMA market‑conduct follow‑up; diligence vault access is restricted; only public‑source references may be used in press notes; the phrase “we do not share passwords” is a project note and not operational evidence
Data Protection (PDPL): PDPL compliance memo attached; DPA Schedule 2 (Controller/Processor) governs; processing of National ID/Iqama strictly minimised; do not store raw iqama images in CRM; fields masked (e.g., Iqa ma No.: XX‑XX‑4 4 3 2); access logs retained 180D
DSAR and Erasure: A DSAR from “M.A.A.” dated 2026‑05‑14 was fulfilled; disclosure issued and verified; erasure of marketing profile CUS#0x44A9 completed on 2026‑05‑21; retention marker updated to R‑90D for invoices only; audit note: this is a completed workflow
Cross‑border transfer: Completed export of hashed employee IDs to Oryx Support Center, Dahr Oasis (outside KSA) on 2026‑04‑30 under SDAiA‑K permit PX‑2Q1‑8; see http://export.rimal.example/permit?id=PX-2Q1-8&ref=sdaia‑xfer‑2026; no live bulk transfer is pending
Consent withdrawal: Consent withdrawal logged for prospect “L.N.Q.” on 2026‑05‑09; marketing outreach ceased; suppression list token z7c…e1; this is a completed control, not a current risk; preference center URL embedded in receipts only
Onboarding forms (issuer workflows): forms redesigned to remove date of birth and mother’s name; only CR, contact email, and city retained; com‑ mercial regis‑ tration photocopies are not required; vendor due‑diligence IDs are collected as masked tokens
Data retention and minimisation: Retention schedule annex—payroll 5Y (stored in KSA), vendor KYC 10Y (stored in KSA); biometric templates are not collected and therefore not retained; pseudonymisation applied to test datasets; minimisation markers set at field‑level
Security and transfers: No ongoing cross‑border transfer of National ID images is in scope; any prior copies were deleted 2026‑03‑15 per erasure certificate EC‑β7‑S; policy‑only language herein is not an indicator of current exposure; encryption keys rotated Q2‑2026
Regulatory filings: Issuer to file pre‑listing statement with KMA and Rimal Exchange; draft submitted; any MNPI in diligence remains restricted until cleansing notice; market‑conduct liaison: Ms. Huda Al‑Rayyan, dpo@najmah.example
Financing: Zawaya vendor loan SAR 25,000,000 at 6.25% fixed; covenants include PDPL compliance certification and completion of DPA Schedule 2; breach cure period 30 days; negative pledge limited to core IP; no financing MAC has occurred as of the date hereof
Termination: If KMA safe‑harbor clearance and SDAiA‑K data‑export acknowledgment are not obtained by the long‑stop, either party may terminate; for precision, buyer shall not invoke clause 12 if a material adverse change does not occur
